location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

seniority.level: Senior Manager (likely equivalent to Director-adjacent IC or people manager)
level_signals: 12+ years of finance and accounting experience, including management level experience
Comp range $192K–$240K base consistent with Senior Manager level at Airbnb

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years of finance and accounting experience, including management level experience in a large multinational organization.
must_haves.qualifier: including management level experience in a large multinational organization

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of relevant industry experience (tech, payments, marketplace(s), hospitality) in a high growth environment.
Strong working knowledge of US GAAP. Proven track record of operationalizing technical accounting standards to satisfy business requirements.
Experience with large-scale ERP (Oracle, SAP, Workday), monetization and billing systems.
Polished communication skills and executive presence. Capable of translating complex business matters across functions and levels of leadership in a clear and concise manner.
Aptitude for collaboration and the ability to influence, mentor, and leverage the skills of others to achieve objectives.
Proven ability to successfully manage complex initiatives with cross-functional ownership.
Champion the team's AI enablement agenda by identifying, piloting, and scaling AI use cases across close, reporting, and reconciliation processes.
Design and maintain appropriate human-review checkpoints for all AI-assisted workflows, ensuring outputs meet audit-ready standards and SOX control requirements.
Partner cross-functionally with Finance leadership, BizTech, and the broader AI enablement initiative to develop accounting-specific use cases, training, and governance frameworks.
Passion for and proven track record of training and developing team members.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: CPA or equivalent international certification, with Big 4 accounting firm experience, a plus.
Payments compliance and reconciliation system experience is a plus.
Experience in SQL and Tableau a plus

scope.surface: Platform Revenue Operations — Invoicing, Accounts Receivable, Bad Debt, Gift Card program, Collections; SOX controls; AI enablement for close and reporting
primary_metrics_inferred: Accuracy and completeness of platform account balances
AI use case adoption across close and reporting processes
core_responsibilities: Partner closely with finance teams to identify, assess and articulate the financial impact(s) of business changes.
Improve visibility for finance leadership into platform accounts and initiatives.
Partner with the platform SOX team to ensure risk identification and control mitigations are appropriate - design, implement, document and review controls to ensure they are functioning properly.
Develop relationships with key stakeholders in payments, compliance, legal and other partner and finance teams on the importance of engaging with Platform Controlling.
Lead the Revenue Operations team, and ensure all processes meet the close deadline.
Teach and mentor staff in accounting and finance on issues, including revenue technical accounting, GAAP guidance and best practices for documentation, partner engagement and operational processes.
Champion the team's AI enablement agenda by identifying, piloting, and scaling AI use cases across close, reporting, and reconciliation processes.

compensation.equity_form: This role may also be eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits.

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($192K–$240K base)
Fully remote-US eligible with no stated in-office mandate beyond occasional offsites
Clear ownership areas stated (Invoicing, AR, Bad Debt, Gift Cards, Collections)
AI enablement framed pragmatically — 'AI tools are preparers, not signers' signals a grounded, non-hype culture
Role reports directly to Director, indicating meaningful scope and visibility
red_flags: Remote eligibility is restricted to states where Airbnb has a registered entity — excluded states list is evolving and not embedded in the JD
Visa sponsorship status is not mentioned; work auth requirement is inferred as no-sponsorship given US-entity state restrictions
12+ years hard requirement narrows the candidate pool significantly
CPA listed as 'a plus' but paired with Big 4 experience — ambiguous whether both are preferred together or independently

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years requirements, and team leadership scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship from US state entity restriction language; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: CPA and Big 4 experience are listed together as 'a plus' — unclear if both are required together to qualify as nice-to-have or if either independently counts.

The JD states 'Remote Eligible' but restricts eligibility to states where Airbnb has a registered entity; the excluded states list is not embedded and is described as 'continuously evolving', making the effective remote scope uncertain.
SQL is listed under 'Proficient in MS Excel; Experience in SQL and Tableau a plus' — the sentence structure is ambiguous as to whether SQL is required or preferred; treated as soft/nice-to-have.
